sections.heading: Risk Factors
sections.content:
Age: Bimodal age distribution with peaks at 15-30 years and ≥55 years. The prognosis is worse for patients >60 years. (Category 2A)
Epstein-Barr Virus (EBV) infection: EBV+ disease is more frequent in older patients and in certain CHL subtypes (e.g., mixed cellularity). EBER-ISH testing is recommended at diagnosis. [HODG-1A, MS-20] (Category 2A)
Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) infection: HIV testing is recommended in the workup for HL. HL is an AIDS-defining illness. [HODG-1] (Category 2A)
Immunosuppression: Conditions or treatments that suppress the immune system are associated with an increased risk of HL. (Category 2A)
Family history: Selection of treatment (CMT vs. chemotherapy alone) should be based on family history of cancer or cardiac disease. [HODG-4, HODG-5A] (Category 2A)

cards.strength: Category 2A (uniform NCCN consensus based on lower-level evidence)
cards.detail: Bimodal age distribution with peaks at 15-30 years and ≥55 years. The prognosis is worse for patients >60 years.
EBV+ disease is more frequent in older patients and in certain CHL subtypes (e.g., mixed cellularity). EBER-ISH testing is recommended at diagnosis. [HODG-1A, MS-20]
HIV testing is recommended in the workup for HL. HL is an AIDS-defining illness. [HODG-1]
Conditions or treatments that suppress the immune system are associated with an increased risk of HL.
Selection of treatment (CMT vs. chemotherapy alone) should be based on family history of cancer or cardiac disease. [HODG-4, HODG-5A]
